spec_match.rationale: Page exercises the concept interactively but stops short of touching the real API surface — the underlying spec behaviour is simulated rather than invoked.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP verified both selects after repairing the escaped script: GPU, no-GPU, policy-denied, context-lost, Canvas2D, CPU upload, and placeholder branches update status and stage output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: DevTools MCP verified the see also section now links to the required chromestatus.com/feature/5166674414927872 entry.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the Remove SwiftShader fallback spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (see chromestatus) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

summary: DevTools MCP on Chrome 148 verified the repaired WebGL Failure Handler at /v139/remove-swiftshader-fallback/webgl-failure-handler/?verify=after-render-guard: both selects changed live status/stage output, stale context-loss timers no longer overwrite newer choices, console/network were clean, and /v139/remove-swiftshader-fallback/conformance/?verify=webgl-failure-handler-fix reported 9 PASS / 0 FAIL. The remaining open question is the existing moderate gap around broader spec-surface coverage.
